# OpenTelemetry MySQL Instrumentation

{{ pypi }}

Instrumentation with MySQL that supports the mysql-connector library and is
specified to trace_integration using 'MySQL'.

## Installation

```
pip install opentelemetry-instrumentation-mysql
```

## References

- [OpenTelemetry MySQL Instrumentation](https://opentelemetry-python-contrib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/instrumentation/mysql/mysql.html)
- [OpenTelemetry Project](https://opentelemetry.io/)
- [OpenTelemetry Python Examples](https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-python/tree/main/docs/examples)

modules.name: opentelemetry.instrumentation.mysql
documentation:
MySQL instrumentation supporting [mysql-connector], it can be enabled by
using `MySQLInstrumentor`.

# Usage

```python
import mysql.connector
from opentelemetry.instrumentation.mysql import MySQLInstrumentor

MySQLInstrumentor().instrument()

cnx = mysql.connector.connect(database="MySQL_Database")
cursor = cnx.cursor()
cursor.execute("INSERT INTO test (testField) VALUES (123)")
cursor.close()
cnx.close()
```

# API

[mysql-connector]: https://pypi.org/project/mysql-connector/

Integrate with MySQL Connector/Python library.
https://dev.mysql.com/doc/connector-python/en/

methods.name: opentelemetry.instrumentation.mysql.MySQLInstrumentor._uninstrument
documentation: "Disable MySQL instrumentation

methods.name: opentelemetry.instrumentation.mysql.MySQLInstrumentor.instrument_connection
documentation:
Enable instrumentation in a MySQL connection.

Args:
    connection: The connection to instrument.
    tracer_provider: The optional tracer provider to use. If omitted
        the current globally configured one is used.

Returns:
    An instrumented connection.
pylint:disable=no-self-use

methods.name: opentelemetry.instrumentation.mysql.MySQLInstrumentor.uninstrument_connection
documentation:
Disable instrumentation in a MySQL connection.

Args:
    connection: The connection to uninstrument.

Returns:
    An uninstrumented connection.
